content.claim: Over the past 20 years, the number of SNI students in Hungary has increased by 40%, with autism spectrum disorder (ASD) identification rising from 30 registered students in 2001 to 9,737 by 2023—a threefold increase over the past decade.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Sources indicate the surge reflects both true prevalence change and improved diagnostic methods, but do not quantify the relative contribution of each factor
Causal attribution between improved diagnosis and rising numbers is an inference rather than a settled finding
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
